name: omero-integration
description: Microscopy data management platform. Access images via Python, retrieve datasets, analyze pixels, manage ROIs/annotations, batch processing, for high-content screening and microscopy workflows.
capabilities: Connect to OMERO servers with secure authentication
Navigate hierarchical data structure (Projects, Datasets, Images)
Access raw pixel data as NumPy arrays for analysis
Create and manage regions of interest (ROIs) on images
Store analysis results in OMERO tables and annotations
Execute server-side scripts for batch processing workflows
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill for OMERO scientific imaging platform. All 655 static findings are false positives. Scanner misidentified markdown code block delimiters as shell commands, random number generation as weak crypto, and legitimate API calls as reconnaissance.
valueStatement: This skill provides Python API access to OMERO, a scientific image management platform for microscopy and high-content screening. Connect to OMERO servers, retrieve images, analyze pixel data, and manage annotations programmatically.
userTitle: Access OMERO microscopy data with Python
limitations: Requires access to a running OMERO server with valid credentials
Does not include image processing algorithms (relies on external libraries)
Cannot modify OMERO server configuration or create new users

useCases.title: Manage large image datasets
useCases.description: Organize and retrieve thousands of microscopy images from OMERO repositories for analysis and reporting.

useCases.title: Automate image analysis pipelines
useCases.description: Extract pixel data from time-series images and store measurement results directly in OMERO.

useCases.title: High-content screening workflows
useCases.description: Process plate-based screening data and track results across experimental conditions.

promptTemplates.title: Basic connection
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to connect to an OMERO server using BlitzGateway with environment variables for credentials.

promptTemplates.title: Image retrieval
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Python code to list all images in a specific OMERO dataset and get their pixel dimensions.

promptTemplates.title: Pixel analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I access raw pixel data from an OMERO image as a NumPy array and calculate intensity statistics?

promptTemplates.title: ROI management
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create rectangular ROIs on an image, extract pixel intensities within those regions, and store the results in an OMERO table.

bestPractices: Use context managers (with BlitzGateway) for automatic connection cleanup
Store credentials in environment variables, never hardcode them
Use secure=True parameter for encrypted production connections
Close connections when done to free server resources
antiPatterns: Hardcoding credentials directly in source code
Leaving connections open without using context managers or explicit close()
Processing large datasets without pagination or streaming

faq.question: What is OMERO?
faq.answer: OMERO is an open-source platform for managing, visualizing, and analyzing microscopy images and metadata in scientific research.

faq.question: Do I need an OMERO server?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill requires access to a running OMERO server with valid user credentials to connect and perform operations.

faq.question: What programming language is used?
faq.answer: The skill provides Python examples using the omero-py library (BlitzGateway API) for all operations.

faq.question: Can I process large image datasets?
faq.answer: Yes, use pagination, limit results, and consider server-side scripts to avoid transferring large amounts of data locally.

faq.question: How are credentials handled?
faq.answer: Credentials should be loaded from environment variables (os.environ.get) or secure configuration files, never hardcoded.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: OMERO supports many formats including TIFF, PNG, JPEG, and proprietary microscopy formats through Bio-Formats.
